strengths: Three objects are specific and their real-world contexts are clearly stated (school darkroom enlarger; Theranos âEdisonâ device; family embroidery hoop).
Links to the IA prompt (âimportance of material toolsâ) are consistently explicit and analytically developed across all objects.
Justifications go beyond description, engaging deeply with TOK concepts (bias, scientific method, cultural transmission), supported by published sources and real-world examples.
Use of evidence is robust: quotations, case studies (Sunak photo manipulation; Holmes lawsuits), and references to peer review reinforce analytical claims.
Structure is coherent, with strong transitions and clear thematic focus on âKnowledge & Technologyâ.
weaknesses: Some citations are incomplete or misformatted (missing access dates, inconsistent citation styles).
Occasional typographical and formatting errors (MathML tags in captions; abrupt sentence endings).
A few analytical points could be tighter or more fully articulated (e.g., deeper engagement with AI bias in photo post-processing).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on strand assessments, the exhibition clearly meets the descriptors for the 9â10 band: specific, well-contextualized objects; strong, evidence-based links to the IA prompt; and thorough justification of each objectâs contribution. Minor issuesâchiefly citation formatting, a few typos, and occasional lapses in analytical depthâprevent a perfect score of 10, but do not significantly detract from the overall high quality. A final score of 9 accurately reflects a very strong exhibition that demonstrates excellent understanding and application of TOK concepts while allowing room for those minor improvements.

annotations.comment: Milmo and Staceyâs entry lacks its accessed date, which is essential for online sources. Add this to complete the citation.
Splitting the URL onto a separate line disrupts readability. Incorporate it directly after the source title for coherence.
The student clearly states the IA prompt in the section header but could deepen engagement by restating the question in their own words to frame the exhibition focus more precisely.

The identification of the theme âKnowledge & Technologyâ is concise and appropriately signals the exhibitionâs focus. Consider referencing TOK terminology here to strengthen conceptual alignment.
The object title clearly identifies a specific real-world tool. To preview structure, the student could briefly outline how each object will link back to the prompt in this introductory line.
The historical context for the enlarger is informative, but the student should provide a precise citation for Henry Fox Talbotâs invention date to support this claim.
The Barthes quotation is well chosen to introduce bias removal, but the footnote citation is misformatted and incomplete. Revise the reference to include author, date, and page number consistently.
Linking the mechanical nature of the enlarger to judicial photo evidence is a creative real-world connection that deepens understanding of material tools in law.
The discussion of AI-enhanced image manipulation is pertinent, but the student should clarify how specific post-processing tools introduce bias into seemingly objective photographs.
The second object is clearly introduced with context. The student effectively situates the Theranos device in a specific real-world scenario from 2015.
The image caption contains stray MathML tags and an unclear citation format. Revise to remove markup errors and clarify source details.
The discussion on peer review would benefit from a concrete example of a failed peer-review process to illustrate the complementary role of material tools and human oversight.
The URL footnote is comprehensive but differs in style from other citations. Standardize formatting to ensure uniformity across the exhibition.
The explanation of Edisonâs fraudulent use effectively links tool accuracy to knowledge production in the natural sciences. Consider adding a specific case study citation for Holmesâs lawsuits.
Relying on an unnamed teacherâs comment weakens the academic rigor. Incorporate a peer-reviewed source to discuss data accuracy relative to material tools.
Combining multiple references in one footnote reduces clarity. Separate citations so each source is clearly attributed.
The transitional sentence summarizing peer reviewâs importance reinforces the analysis effectively before introducing object 3.
Including a personal photograph of the embroidery hoop strengthens authenticity and personal engagement in the exhibition narrative.
The origin story of the embroidery hoop is insightful but lacks external context. Adding a brief history of the toolâs development in textile craft would strengthen justification.
The final sentence in this paragraph is incomplete and cuts off abruptly. Ensure each analytical point is fully articulated to maintain clarity.
The critique of structuralist feminist perspectives demonstrates critical engagement with knowledge frameworks.
When discussing Betsy Greerâs craftivism, including a direct quotation from her work would bolster the sociological argument.
The single-line footnote lacks author and publication details. Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., Harvard) for all footnotes.
The bibliography is thorough and demonstrates wide research, supporting the strength of the exhibitionâs evidential base.
The Fitzgerald entry is detailed but the style deviates from Harvard conventions. Include uniform punctuation and parenthetical access dates.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
